sections.html:
Design Movement · International Style

Swiss Graphic Style

Radical clarity through mathematical precision — the Swiss Graphic Style strips design to its purest essentials: sans-serif type, strict grid alignment, generous whitespace, and a single bold accent color against monochrome. Born in the 1950s at Basel and Zurich design schools, it proved that impeccable typography, systematic grids, and disciplined restraint could create designs of timeless power.

Swiss Graphic Style is the foundation of corporate identity worldwide — you'll see its DNA in airline branding, museum signage systems, and multinational corporate identities. It appears on architecture firm sites, design education platforms, and typographic specimen pages — anywhere that treats systematic clarity as the highest design virtue.
